forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,78 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: '8680'
+project: ai-panel
+title: 'E2E test: prove llama-agent works through the full kanban-app stack (qwen-0.6b-test)'
+---
+## Why
+
+The ai-panel project shipped tasks 1ŌĆō4 with green "all tests pass" reports, but **two bugs only surfaced under manual testing**:
+
+1. `update.board` was an unknown command (the picker dispatch silently failed; model never persisted). Tests were mock-based at the dispatch boundary.
+2. Qwen-27B returned 0 tokens (the model loaded but generated nothing ŌĆö the actual chat path is broken). No test exercised real generation.
+
+Mocked unit tests at the dispatch boundary + unit tests at the operation layer aren't enough. We need a **real-path smoke test** that drives an actual llama-agent model through the production code paths and asserts the user-visible contract: a non-empty response.
+
+The user explicitly flagged this gap: *"i want my manual testing to be more quality focused and not the lame works-at-all you just subjected me to"*.
+
+## What
+
+Build an integration test in `apps/kanban-app/tests/` (or wherever Tauri-side integration tests live ŌĆö check the existing test layout) that drives the **production code path** from `ai_start_agent` through to a returned response:
+
+1. **Use `qwen-0.6b-test.yaml`** ŌĆö the small qwen model that's already in `builtin/models/`. The 27B model is impractical for CI (downloads ~14 GB, runs slow on every developer machine). 0.6B downloads in seconds and generates in milliseconds.
+2. **Wire it through the real `ai_list_models` / `ai_start_agent` / `resolve_model_config`** ŌĆö call the same Tauri commands the webview calls. Not a mock, not a unit test of `llama_agent::create_agent`.
+3. **Send a real `session/prompt`** over ACP (or the in-process equivalent) and assert `tokens_generated > 0`.
+4. **Send a second prompt in the same session** and assert it doesn't fail with "Queue is full" ŌĆö the queue-lifecycle regression from bug `01KSNJ7CBK9333J0T9G4TCA7DH`.
+5. **Send a prompt that should call the kanban MCP tool** (e.g. ask the model to count tasks) and assert the tool was invoked at least once. This guards the MCP wiring path ŌĆö that the board's `mcpUrl` makes it into `newSession.mcpServers` and the tool is reachable from the model.
+
+If `qwen-0.6b-test.yaml` isn't currently tagged `kanban`, that's fine ŌĆö the test doesn't need the tag to drive `ai_start_agent` directly with the model id. Do NOT add the `kanban` tag to `qwen-0.6b-test.yaml` (it would clutter the user-facing picker).
+
+### What this test must NOT do
+
+- It must NOT use mocks for `useDispatchCommand`, `create_agent`, `ai_start_agent`, or any other boundary. The whole point is to drive the production path.
+- It must NOT just assert "no error was thrown". 0 tokens generated is a successful API call with a broken result. Assert on the **content**: token count, response non-empty, tool calls made.
+- It must NOT be skipped on CI behind an env var or a flaky-test tag. If it's flaky, fix the underlying flake. A test that's not run is the same as no test.
+
+### Performance budget
+
+The user's session showed Qwen3.6-27B taking 2:02 from prompt to (empty) response. With qwen-0.6b-test the round-trip should be seconds. Hard cap: **30s** for the full test (including first-time model download). If it exceeds that, investigate ŌĆö that's a signal something is wrong with cold-start.
+
+### File layout suggestion
+
+- `apps/kanban-app/tests/ai_panel_e2e.rs` ŌĆö the test (or extend an existing integration test file if there's already one for kanban-app).
+- Use the `#[tokio::test]` attribute and the existing TempDir + KanbanContext setup pattern from `tests/per_board_model_isolation.rs`.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+
+- [ ] A new integration test exists in `apps/kanban-app/tests/` that drives `ai_start_agent` with the literal id `qwen-0.6b-test` (or whatever id `qwen-0.6b-test.yaml` declares) and ends up with a non-empty model response.
+- [ ] The test asserts `tokens_generated > 0` (NOT just "no error" ŌĆö that's the failure mode bug `01KSNJ7CBK9333J0T9G4TCA7DH` would still pass).
+- [ ] The test asserts the queue can accept a second prompt after the first completes (queue-lifecycle guard).
+- [ ] The test asserts the kanban MCP tool was reachable ŌĆö easiest version: assert the tool was advertised in the session (Ōēź1 tool from the `kanban` MCP server). Stretch: actually make the model call a kanban tool and assert the tool result was returned.
+- [ ] If this test had existed before bug `01KSNJ6AE18EQYDC2WSYFSSAY1` and bug `01KSNJ7CBK9333J0T9G4TCA7DH` were introduced, it would have failed.
+- [ ] Runs under 30 seconds on a developer machine (post first-time model download).
+- [ ] Runs in CI (no env-var gate, no `#[ignore]`).
+
+## Tests
+
+- [ ] `test_ai_panel_e2e_qwen_small_model_generates_tokens` ŌĆö the core test above.
+- [ ] `test_ai_panel_e2e_second_prompt_queues_after_first_completes` ŌĆö queue-lifecycle guard.
+- [ ] `test_ai_panel_e2e_mcp_tool_visible_in_session` ŌĆö MCP wiring guard.
+- [ ] Run: `cargo test -p kanban-app --test ai_panel_e2e`.
+
+## Workflow
+
+- Start by **reading** the production path end to end: `apps/kanban-app/src/ai/models.rs` (`ai_list_models`, `ai_start_agent`, `resolve_model_config`), `apps/kanban-app/src/ai/agent_ws.rs` (`AgentWebSocketServer::bind_with`), and the llama-agent entry points it calls. Understand exactly what runs in production before deciding which seam to test from.
+- Pick the **highest seam that still exercises real code** ŌĆö likely `ai_start_agent` returning an `AgentEndpoint`, then opening an ACP WebSocket client against the returned `wsUrl`, then driving real ACP `session/new` + `session/prompt`. Drive ACP from the test, not just internal Rust calls ŌĆö that's the same path the webview uses.
+- Use `/tdd` ŌĆö write the failing assertions first.
+- If the test surfaces additional bugs (e.g. discovers that `qwen-0.6b-test` itself also generates 0 tokens), document them as new tasks and tag this one stuck ŌĆö do NOT silently relax the assertions to "works at all". The whole point of this card is that "works at all" is not good enough.
+
+## Lesson this codifies
+
+A green "all tests pass" report means nothing if no test exercises the path the user hits. Every user-visible feature deserves at least one production-path test that would have caught the kind of regression manual testing finds. Mock-heavy unit tests are a complement to those, not a replacement.
+
+## Related
+
+- Bug `01KSNJ6AE18EQYDC2WSYFSSAY1` (fixed in commit `07c3c6583`): `update.board` unknown command. This test would have caught it because picking a model triggers `update.board` and the test would verify it succeeded.
+- Bug `01KSNJ7CBK9333J0T9G4TCA7DH` (open): qwen generates 0 tokens. This test would have caught it on the first prompt assertion.
\ No newline at end of file

-Mocked unit tests at the dispatch boundary + unit tests at the operation layer aren't enough. We need a **real-path smoke test** that drives an actual llama-agent model through the production code paths and asserts the user-visible contract: a non-empty response.

-
-Build an integration test in `apps/kanban-app/tests/` (or wherever Tauri-side integration tests live ŌĆö check the existing test layout) that drives the **production code path** from `ai_start_agent` through to a returned response:

-3. **Send a real `session/prompt`** over ACP (or the in-process equivalent) and assert `tokens_generated > 0`.
-4. **Send a second prompt in the same session** and assert it doesn't fail with "Queue is full" ŌĆö the queue-lifecycle regression from bug `01KSNJ7CBK9333J0T9G4TCA7DH`.
-5. **Send a prompt that should call the kanban MCP tool** (e.g. ask the model to count tasks) and assert the tool was invoked at least once. This guards the MCP wiring path ŌĆö that the board's `mcpUrl` makes it into `newSession.mcpServers` and the tool is reachable from the model.
-
-If `qwen-0.6b-test.yaml` isn't currently tagged `kanban`, that's fine ŌĆö the test doesn't need the tag to drive `ai_start_agent` directly with the model id. Do NOT add the `kanban` tag to `qwen-0.6b-test.yaml` (it would clutter the user-facing picker).
-

-- It must NOT use mocks for `useDispatchCommand`, `create_agent`, `ai_start_agent`, or any other boundary. The whole point is to drive the production path.
-- It must NOT just assert "no error was thrown". 0 tokens generated is a successful API call with a broken result. Assert on the **content**: token count, response non-empty, tool calls made.
-- It must NOT be skipped on CI behind an env var or a flaky-test tag. If it's flaky, fix the underlying flake. A test that's not run is the same as no test.

-### Performance budget
-
-The user's session showed Qwen3.6-27B taking 2:02 from prompt to (empty) response. With qwen-0.6b-test the round-trip should be seconds. Hard cap: **30s** for the full test (including first-time model download). If it exceeds that, investigate ŌĆö that's a signal something is wrong with cold-start.
-
-### File layout suggestion
-
-- `apps/kanban-app/tests/ai_panel_e2e.rs` ŌĆö the test (or extend an existing integration test file if there's already one for kanban-app).
-- Use the `#[tokio::test]` attribute and the existing TempDir + KanbanContext setup pattern from `tests/per_board_model_isolation.rs`.
-
-## Acceptance Criteria
-
-- [ ] A new integration test exists in `apps/kanban-app/tests/` that drives `ai_start_agent` with the literal id `qwen-0.6b-test` (or whatever id `qwen-0.6b-test.yaml` declares) and ends up with a non-empty model response.
-- [ ] The test asserts `tokens_generated > 0` (NOT just "no error" ŌĆö that's the failure mode bug `01KSNJ7CBK9333J0T9G4TCA7DH` would still pass).
-- [ ] The test asserts the queue can accept a second prompt after the first completes (queue-lifecycle guard).
-- [ ] The test asserts the kanban MCP tool was reachable ŌĆö easiest version: assert the tool was advertised in the session (Ōēź1 tool from the `kanban` MCP server). Stretch: actually make the model call a kanban tool and assert the tool result was returned.
-- [ ] If this test had existed before bug `01KSNJ6AE18EQYDC2WSYFSSAY1` and bug `01KSNJ7CBK9333J0T9G4TCA7DH` were introduced, it would have failed.
-- [ ] Runs under 30 seconds on a developer machine (post first-time model download).
-- [ ] Runs in CI (no env-var gate, no `#[ignore]`).
-
-## Tests
-
-- [ ] `test_ai_panel_e2e_qwen_small_model_generates_tokens` ŌĆö the core test above.
-- [ ] `test_ai_panel_e2e_second_prompt_queues_after_first_completes` ŌĆö queue-lifecycle guard.
-- [ ] `test_ai_panel_e2e_mcp_tool_visible_in_session` ŌĆö MCP wiring guard.
-- [ ] Run: `cargo test -p kanban-app --test ai_panel_e2e`.

-- Start by **reading** the production path end to end: `apps/kanban-app/src/ai/models.rs` (`ai_list_models`, `ai_start_agent`, `resolve_model_config`), `apps/kanban-app/src/ai/agent_ws.rs` (`AgentWebSocketServer::bind_with`), and the llama-agent entry points it calls. -- Pick the **highest seam that still exercises real code** ŌĆö likely `ai_start_agent` returning an `AgentEndpoint`, then opening an ACP WebSocket client against the returned `wsUrl`, then driving real ACP `session/new` + `session/prompt`. Drive ACP from the test, not just internal Rust calls ŌĆö that's the same path the webview uses.
-- Use `/tdd` ŌĆö write the failing assertions first.
-- If the test surfaces additional bugs (e.g. discovers that `qwen-0.6b-test` itself also generates 0 tokens), document them as new tasks and tag this one stuck ŌĆö do NOT silently relax the assertions to "works at all". The whole point of this card is that "works at all" is not good enough.

-A green "all tests pass" report means nothing if no test exercises the path the user hits. Every user-visible feature deserves at least one production-path test that would have caught the kind of regression manual testing finds. Mock-heavy unit tests are a complement to those, not a replacement.
